text: Fill in the blanks about the physiology of special senses.
questions: The retina contains two types of photoreceptors: *rods* (for dim light and peripheral vision) and *cones* (for colour and fine detail).
Sound is transmitted from the tympanic membrane through three ossicles: *malleus*, incus, and *stapes* to the oval window.
The organ of Corti in the *cochlea* contains hair cells that are the receptors for hearing.
Taste sensation from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue is carried by the *facial* nerve (CN VII), while the posterior one-third is carried by the *glossopharyngeal* nerve (CN IX).
The semicircular canals detect *rotational/angular* acceleration, while the utricle and saccule detect *linear* acceleration and head position.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
